title: paraboea obtusa
intro: Paraboea obtusa is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae, commonly known as the obtuse paraboea or the toothless paraboea. It is a small, terrestrial plant native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, and its medicinal properties lie in its leaves, which are traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The leaves of Paraboea obtusa can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and are also used in traditional herbal medicine, often in combination with other plants, to enhance its therapeutic effects.
